back_translation: At that time there were some who came down from the region of Judea and taught the brothers, "If you do not receive circumcision according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved."

When there arose severe dispute and debate between Paul and Barnabas and those men, the church appointed Paul, Barnabas, and some other brothers to go up to Jerusalem to meet the apostles and elders of the church concerning this question.
When the church had sent them off on their journey, they passed through the regions of Phoenicia and Samaria, recounting the conversion of the Gentiles, causing all the brothers to have great joy.
When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders of the church welcomed them, and they reported everything that God had done together with them.
But some in the group of Pharisees who had believed stood up and said, "It is necessary that the Gentiles be circumcised, and they must be commanded to observe the law of Moses."
The apostles and the elders of the church met to consider this matter.
After much debate, Peter stood up and said to them, "Brothers, you know that long ago God chose me from among you, so that the Gentiles might hear the word of the gospel from my mouth and believe.
And God who knows the hearts of men was a witness on their behalf, by giving the Holy Spirit to them just as He gave to us.
He made no distinction between us and them, for He cleansed their hearts to be pure by faith.
Now then, why do you challenge God by placing a yoke on the necks of the disciples that neither our ancestors nor we ourselves have been able to bear?
But we believe that we are saved by the grace of the Lord Jesus, just as they also are saved."
Then the whole assembly fell silent and listened to Barnabas and Paul recount the signs and wonders that God had done among the Gentiles through them.
When the two of them had finished speaking, James responded, saying, "Brothers, listen to me.
Simeon has told how God first visited the Gentiles, to choose a people from among them for His name.
And the words of the prophets agree with this matter, as it is written:
'After these things I will return and rebuild the fallen tent of David; its ruins I will rebuild and set it up firmly,
so that the remnant of men may seek the Lord, and all the Gentiles who have been called by My name, says the Lord who does these things,
which have been known since ancient times.'
Therefore I judge that we should not add burden to the Gentiles who are turning to God,
but write a letter to them to abstain from things defiled by idols, from sexual immorality, from the meat of animals strangled to death, and from blood.
For since ancient times, in every city there have been those proclaiming the law of Moses, and it is read in the synagogues every Sabbath day."

At that time the apostles, the elders of the church, together with the whole church, thought it fitting to choose some men from among them to send to the city of Antioch together with Paul and Barnabas, namely Judas who was called Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers.

They wrote a letter entrusted to the hands of the two men, saying, "The apostles and the elders of the church, your brothers, to the Gentile brothers who are in the city of Antioch, the region of Syria, and the region of Cilicia: may well-being be with you all.
We have heard that some from among us went out and caused disturbance to you by their words, making your minds confused, though we did not command them in any way.
When we had become unanimous in agreement, we thought it fitting to choose men to send to you along with Barnabas and Paul, our beloved ones,
who have handed over their own lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Therefore we are sending Judas and Silas, so that the two of them may tell you by word of mouth the same matter.
For it seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us to place no burden upon you beyond these necessary things:

that you abstain from things sacrificed to idols, from blood, from the meat of animals strangled to death, and from sexual immorality. If you keep yourselves from these things, it will be well with you. Farewell."
When they had been sent off, they went down to the city of Antioch, and gathering together the congregation of believers, they delivered the letter.
When the letter had been read, the brothers rejoiced because of the encouraging words in the letter.
Judas and Silas, who were already prophets, spoke many words to encourage and strengthen the brothers.
After spending a time there, the brothers sent the two of them back in peace, to those who had sent them.
As for Paul and Barnabas, they remained in the city of Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord together with many others.
Afterwards, after a few days, Paul said to Barnabas, "Let us return and visit the brothers in every city where we have proclaimed the word of the Lord, to see how they are doing."
As for Barnabas, he wished to take John who was called Mark along with them.
But Paul did not think it right to take Mark along with them, because he had once abandoned them in the region of Pamphylia and had not accompanied them in the work.
There arose such a sharp dispute that the two of them separated from each other; Barnabas then took Mark and boarded a ship to the island of Cyprus.
As for Paul, he chose Silas and departed, the brothers having committed him to the grace of the Lord.
Paul and Silas traveled through the region of Syria and the region of Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
